extraction.one_line_summary: IMPEY Daryl won by refusing to pull in final kilometer after TEUNISSEN Tijs did all the work, then outsprinting him despite being slower on paper.
tactical_patterns: POSTLBERGER Lucas solo attacked the 14-rider break early and extended advantage despite cooperation behind, showing breakaway hesitation vulnerability.
IMPEY Daryl sat on TEUNISSEN's wheel entire final kilometer, forcing Belgian to set tempo then outsprinting him easily.
ROCHE Nicolas sat in breakaway group refusing pulls, causing chase demoralization and accelerating group disintegration behind leaders.

rider_signals.POSTLBERGER Lucas: positive: sustained solo advantage over cooperating 14-rider chase group including powerful rouleurs, exceptional time-trialing strength
rider_signals.IMPEY Daryl: positive: tactically superior in 2-up sprint, let TEUNISSEN do all work final kilometer despite being labeled slower rider
rider_signals.TEUNISSEN Tijs: negative: lacked tactical awareness to force IMPEY through in final kilometer, did all pacemaking and lost sprint
rider_signals.ROCHE Nicolas: neutral: refused pulls in chase group but stayed with breakaway, tactical sitting-on demoralized chasers
